questions.question: Describe the problem that the Bayer filter introduces and the process used to solve it. (3 marks)
keyPoints: The Bayer filter means each photosite only captures one color (R, G, or B).
This results in a mosaic image where each pixel location is missing two color values.
The solution is demosaicing, an interpolation algorithm that estimates the missing color values for each pixel based on its neighbors.

questions.question: Explain the entire image formation process inside a digital camera, starting with light entering the lens and ending with a stored JPEG file. Discuss the role of at least four key components. (10 marks)
keyPoints: Light from the scene enters the lens, which focuses it onto the sensor plane.
The image sensor (CCD/CMOS) contains photosites that convert photons into an analog electrical charge.
A Bayer Filter Array over the sensor allows each photosite to record only red, green, or blue light.
An Analog-to-Digital Converter (ADC) quantizes the analog signal into discrete digital values (defines bit depth).
Demosaicing interpolates the Bayer pattern data to create a full RGB value for every pixel.
Post-processing applies white balance, gamma correction, and noise reduction.
The image is compressed (e.g., using JPEG algorithm) and written to a storage medium.
